A signal must be heavily filtered to remove a narrowband tone before entering a sensitive measurement circuit. The passbands span below 14.4 kHz and above 55.1 kHz with a maximum of 11.1 dB loss. At least 82.1 dB of suppression is needed between 19 kHz and 27.2 kHz. Only passive R and C components may be used; keep the component count low.

REQUIREMENTS:
  Type: notch filter
  Notch centre: 22.74 kHz
  Notch bandwidth (-3 dB): 19 kHz – 27.22 kHz
  Stopband sample (fs): 21.76 kHz  [min attenuation: 82.1 dB]

```spice
* Notch Filter Design for Removing Narrowband Tone

* Input Voltage Source
V1 VIN GND AC 1

* Resistor and Capacitor Values Calculation
* Center Frequency (f0) = 22.74 kHz
* Bandwidth (BW) = 27.22 kHz - 19 kHz = 8.22 kHz
* Quality Factor (Q) = f0 / BW = 22.74 kHz / 8.22 kHz ≈ 2.75

* Using the formula for R and C in a notch filter:
* R = 1 / (2 * π * f0 * Q)
* C = 1 / (2 * π * f0 * R)

* Calculate R and C
R1 1 2 1000  ; Example value, adjust as necessary
C1 2 GND 10uF ; Example value, adjust as necessary

* Output Node
VOUT 1 GND

.END
```
